_note: Updated by the support agent after each task. Plain language — no jargon.

priorities.title: Document the intended first-session experience so we know what success looks like for new users
priorities.note: We can't help users who get stuck if we haven't defined what a successful first session is. The target: new user opens the tool and exports a room within their first session. Every friction point before that export is a support problem.
priorities.proposed_solution: Document the intended path from first open to first room export and list support triggers at each friction point.

priorities.title: Write a FAQ covering the most common questions early testers have asked
priorities.note: Without a FAQ, the same questions get answered repeatedly. Getting the top five questions documented saves time and reduces early-adopter frustration.
priorities.proposed_solution: Publish a short FAQ for the top early-tester questions across itch, GitHub README, and in-tool help.

actions.name: Feedback Synthesis
actions.description: Tagged product intelligence from a batch of user feedback — issue types by frequency, JTBD failures, top user quote worth seeing.
actions.trigger: Given a batch of user feedback, tickets, or community comments

actions.name: Onboarding Audit
actions.description: First-session experience map — where do new users stall before first export? Specific friction points with recommended fixes.
actions.trigger: Monthly or when activation rate drops

actions.name: FAQ Update
actions.description: Updated documentation for itch.io page, GitHub README, and in-tool help text based on recent support patterns.
actions.trigger: After any product change or support volume surge on a specific topic

opportunities.title: Set up a feedback intake channel before public launch
opportunities.note: Before external users arrive, we need a defined place for them to report issues and leave feedback. Itch.io comments and a GitHub issue tracker are the most appropriate channels for this community.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Before public users arrive, designate itch and GitHub as official feedback channels with clear templates and response expectations.

opportunities.title: Create a bug report escalation workflow from support to QA
opportunities.note: When users report bugs, there's currently no defined path to get those reports into QA's triage process with enough reproduction detail to be actionable.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Define how user bug reports include repro steps and route into QA triage with owners and severity.
